title: libpod: support for cgroup namespace
body:
allow a container to run in a new cgroup namespace.

When running in a new cgroup namespace, the current cgroup appears to be the root, so that there is no way for the container to access cgroups outside of its own subtree.

By default it uses --cgroup=host to keep the previous behavior.

To create a new namespace, --cgroup=private must be provided.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

I think pods also need to share CGroups

> I think pods also need to share CGroups

by default we don't create a new cgroup namespace, so that containers still see the full path.
@mheon @rhatdan do we care to wait until Docker decides on the name?
We already need to do flag normalization for healthchecks; I don't really mind doing the same here if they decide on a different name.
